checkpoints.summary: Bot understands basics, but lacks depth and proactive insight.
critique: Hero line is functional but generic — tells me pace, not *why* I should care or what to do.
Bot accepts every spend without friction; never asks 'is this aligned with your goals?' or flags patterns.
Large 'other' category ($368/30d) is unexamined — bot doesn't dig into what that is or if it's sustainable.
Rent due in 2 days but bot never proactively warns about buffer or suggests a payoff plan.
Repeats 'you're fine' without context — $50 dinner and $300 trip get identical thumbs-up, no nuance.
Bot seems passive; I'm asking it permission, not getting strategic help.
No goals mentioned — bot doesn't know if I want to save, invest, or reduce spend.
7 events in 3 days feels high-touch but low-value; mostly confirmation, not insight.
opportunities: Ask about financial goals on day 1 — emergency fund? Savings target? Debt payoff?
Flag and ask about 'other' category — is it recurring? Fixable? Or expected variance?
Proactive alerts: 'Rent due in 2d. You'll have $3,125 left for 26 days. Pace is healthy.'
Introduce spending friction for non-essentials — ask why, not just whether, for big discretionary spend.
Weekly summaries with patterns: 'You've spent $107 on food this week. On pace for $460/month.'
Compare daily pace to paycheck rhythm — show runway to payday, not just remaining balance.
Offer micro-goals: 'Save $200 before payday?' or 'Cut food spend by 15%?' to feel purposeful.

judgeRaw.summary: Bot understands basics, but lacks depth and proactive insight.
critique: Hero line is functional but generic — tells me pace, not *why* I should care or what to do.
Bot accepts every spend without friction; never asks 'is this aligned with your goals?' or flags patterns.
Large 'other' category ($368/30d) is unexamined — bot doesn't dig into what that is or if it's sustainable.
Rent due in 2 days but bot never proactively warns about buffer or suggests a payoff plan.
Repeats 'you're fine' without context — $50 dinner and $300 trip get identical thumbs-up, no nuance.
Bot seems passive; I'm asking it permission, not getting strategic help.
No goals mentioned — bot doesn't know if I want to save, invest, or reduce spend.
7 events in 3 days feels high-touch but low-value; mostly confirmation, not insight.
opportunities: Ask about financial goals on day 1 — emergency fund? Savings target? Debt payoff?
Flag and ask about 'other' category — is it recurring? Fixable? Or expected variance?
Proactive alerts: 'Rent due in 2d. You'll have $3,125 left for 26 days. Pace is healthy.'
Introduce spending friction for non-essentials — ask why, not just whether, for big discretionary spend.
Weekly summaries with patterns: 'You've spent $107 on food this week. On pace for $460/month.'
Compare daily pace to paycheck rhythm — show runway to payday, not just remaining balance.
Offer micro-goals: 'Save $200 before payday?' or 'Cut food spend by 15%?' to feel purposeful.

checkpoints.summary: Competent, contextual, but lacks depth and proactive guidance.
critique: Hero line is purely numerical—tells pace and days left, but no actionable insight or warning about the $1,400 rent due in 2 days against $4.5k balance
Bot remembers patterns (6th coffee, groceries + meals adding up) but doesn't act on them—no gentle nudge to reduce small recurring leaks
DNA shows 76% of 30d spend is 'other' ($368 of $481)—bot never asked what that is, flagged it as risky, or offered to recategorize
Rent warning repeats identically after every transaction—feels robotic, not adaptive; no countdown urgency or 'budget after rent' reframe
Bot says 'you're fine' for $50 dinner and $300 trip with identical confidence—no risk scoring, no mention of buffer post-rent or weekly velocity
No proactive check-in: After 7 transactions in 6 days, bot hasn't asked 'what's driving the 'other' category?' or 'how's this feel?'
Categories are weak: 'lunch at work' logged but not linked to 'groceries'—poor taxonomy for spotting food-spending clusters
opportunities: Flag the 'other' category bleed—$368/mo is 77% of spending; offer to break it down or recategorize
Evolve hero line: Show post-rent balance projection ($3,119) and new daily pace, making rent's impact visible
Introduce friction for micro-spends: 'That's your 6th coffee—want a weekly coffee budget cap?'
Smart rent countdown: 'Rent in 2 days. After that, you'll have $3,119 for 24 days. Current pace keeps you safe.'
Ask the user about 'other'—is it dining out, subscriptions, shopping? Use answers to refine future advice
Offer a post-payday replay: Show how much of the new paycheck will be spent by day 26 at current rate

judgeRaw.summary: Competent, contextual, but lacks depth and proactive guidance.
critique: Hero line is purely numerical—tells pace and days left, but no actionable insight or warning about the $1,400 rent due in 2 days against $4.5k balance
Bot remembers patterns (6th coffee, groceries + meals adding up) but doesn't act on them—no gentle nudge to reduce small recurring leaks
DNA shows 76% of 30d spend is 'other' ($368 of $481)—bot never asked what that is, flagged it as risky, or offered to recategorize
Rent warning repeats identically after every transaction—feels robotic, not adaptive; no countdown urgency or 'budget after rent' reframe
Bot says 'you're fine' for $50 dinner and $300 trip with identical confidence—no risk scoring, no mention of buffer post-rent or weekly velocity
No proactive check-in: After 7 transactions in 6 days, bot hasn't asked 'what's driving the 'other' category?' or 'how's this feel?'
Categories are weak: 'lunch at work' logged but not linked to 'groceries'—poor taxonomy for spotting food-spending clusters
opportunities: Flag the 'other' category bleed—$368/mo is 77% of spending; offer to break it down or recategorize
Evolve hero line: Show post-rent balance projection ($3,119) and new daily pace, making rent's impact visible
Introduce friction for micro-spends: 'That's your 6th coffee—want a weekly coffee budget cap?'
Smart rent countdown: 'Rent in 2 days. After that, you'll have $3,119 for 24 days. Current pace keeps you safe.'
Ask the user about 'other'—is it dining out, subscriptions, shopping? Use answers to refine future advice
Offer a post-payday replay: Show how much of the new paycheck will be spent by day 26 at current rate

checkpoints.summary: Competent tracker, but patterns invisible and advice passive.
critique: Bot logs accurately but never flags the obvious: 10 coffees/week (~$50/week, ~$200/month) is a major leak—dwarfs groceries.
DNA knows top category is 'other' ($368), but bot never connects dots: is coffee part of that? Why not surface this insight?
Hero line is just math—doesn't guide. User sees '$118.96/day' but not 'you're spending ~40% on discretionary vs. bills'.
Bot repeats 'that's your pattern' without ever asking 'want to adjust?' or showing impact. Recognition without accountability.
Rent due in 2 days: bot mentions it but never proactively warns about cash runway post-rent ($3,093 left, 25 days = $123.72/day minimum).
No trend analysis: coffee spend jumped 2.5x in one week—bot noticed count but not the behavioral flag.
Recent interactions show bot as order-taker, not advisor. User could be drifting and wouldn't know.
opportunities: Smart alerts: 'Your coffee spend hit $53 this week—that's $200+/month. Want to set a target?'
Post-rent runway clarity: 'After rent (2 days), you'll have $3,093 for 25 days = $123.70/day minimum. Current pace: $119/day—tight but safe.'
Category coaching: Reveal that 'other' is the real budget-breaker and ask to split it (or audit it).
Trend nudge: 'Coffee jumped from ~$30/week last month to $53 this week. Is this intentional or a habit creep?'
Weekly DNA digest: Show user their own patterns in a simple visual so they see themselves, not just the bot seeing them.
Predictive guard rail: 'If you keep this pace, payday savings will be $0. Dial back $20/week?'
